headline: CPI Cools, Banks Post Record Earnings; SK Hynix ADRs Surge on HBM Outlook
summary: US equities rallied on June's cooling inflation data, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing higher. Major banks reported record earnings, while SK Hynix ADRs jumped over 27% on strong HBM sentiment. Geopolitical shifts saw Trump reverse Hormuz fee demands, impacting oil prices.

bullets.lead: US equities rallied as June inflation data cooled, easing rate hike concerns.

segments.text: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed higher following a slowdown in inflation to 3.5% in June, driven by a break in gasoline prices and solid bank earnings.

sources.title: [3분증시] 뉴욕증시, 한풀 꺾인 CPI에 안도 랠리
Cooling Inflation, Surging Bank Earnings Power Stocks
S&P 500 and Nasdaq end higher on cool inflation data, solid bank earnings

bullets.lead: Fed officials reiterated commitment to inflation targets despite recent data.

segments.text: Fed Governor Kevin Warsh affirmed the central bank's pledge to reduce inflation and stated "no tolerance" for high inflation, though without specifying new policy actions.

sources.title: Warsh Reiterates Fed’s Pledge to Get Inflation Down but Doesn’t Specify How
Warsh Tells Congress the Fed Has ‘No Tolerance’ for High Inflation

bullets.lead: SK Hynix ADRs surged on strong analyst sentiment for HBM leadership.

segments.text: Barclays set a $330 price target for SK Hynix ADRs, citing the company's "unbeatable" position in HBM, leading to a 27% jump in ADRs and a nearly 50% premium over Korean shares.

sources.title: 바클레이즈, SK하이닉스ADR 목표가 330 달러 파격 제시..."HBM 넘사벽"
SK Hynix ADR Premium Balloons to Nearly 50% Over Korean Shares

bullets.lead: Samsung is accelerating its semiconductor fab operations in Yongin.

segments.text: Samsung has advanced the start of operations for the first fab in its Yongin semiconductor cluster by two years, now targeting October 2029 for the first of six planned fabs.

bullets.lead: Samsung officially denied rumors of a US ADR listing.

segments.text: Samsung Electronics refuted reports of considering a US ADR listing, stating such plans are "factually groundless" and have not been reviewed.

sources.title: 삼성전자, 美 증시 상장설 일축…"검토 사실무근"

bullets.lead: Nvidia's H200 AI chip shipments to China have reportedly commenced.

segments.text: Reports indicate that Nvidia has begun shipping its H200 AI chips to China, contributing to a 4% rise in the company's stock.

sources.title: “엔비디아 H200 중국 출하 시작”…주가 4% ↑

bullets.lead: Nvidia's next-gen Rubin V platform faces a PCB bottleneck.

segments.text: The Rubin V platform is experiencing delays due to HDI PCB issues from Victory Giant, specifically an impedance mismatch affecting signal integrity, with resolution potentially extending into Q3.

bullets.lead: TSMC plans to increase prices for mature-process chip manufacturing.

segments.text: TSMC intends to implement a single-digit percentage price hike on mature-process chips in Q4, effective January, driven by robust AI boom demand for components like PMICs.

bullets.lead: Apple is accelerating its M-series chip roadmap for AI.

segments.text: Apple is expected to launch a base M6 this fall, skip Pro/Max/Ultra, and move directly to M7 in H1 2027, accelerating due to significant Neural Engine AI upgrades, with M7 Ultra rumored for 2028.

bullets.lead: Trump reversed his demand for a 20% fee on Hormuz cargo.

segments.text: President Trump rescinded the threat of a 20% fee on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz, leading to a dip in oil prices as the US resumed its blockade of Iranian ports.

sources.title: Trump scraps threat of 20% fee on Hormuz cargo as US resumes blockade of Iran ports
Oil Price Dips From High After Trump Changes Plans for Shipping Fees
Trump backtracks on Hormuz 20% toll demand
Trump Shelves 20% Fee for Hormuz Cargo After Gulf Pressure

bullets.lead: China's CXMT is set to raise 15 trillion won through an IPO.

segments.text: ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT) plans an IPO to secure 15 trillion won, which is expected to benefit Chinese semiconductor equipment manufacturers.

sources.title: NH투자증권 "CXMT IPO로 15조원 조달…중국 반도체 장비주 수혜"

bullets.lead: The US is funding Trump-aligned European entities to strengthen "civilisational bonds".

segments.text: The US government is providing funding to European groups aligned with former President Trump, aiming to foster "civilisational bonds" and potentially influence future policy.

sources.title: US to fund Trump-aligned Europeans building ‘civilisational bonds’

bullets.lead: Apple is reportedly suing OpenAI for trade secret theft.

segments.text: Apple has initiated legal action against OpenAI, alleging the theft of trade secrets related to upcoming Apple products and accusing OpenAI of encouraging employees to share confidential information.

sources.title: Apple Suing OpenAI for Stealing Trade Secrets, Former Meta Director of AI on Muse Spark — TITV [Video]

bullets.lead: Major US banks reported record earnings, but future risks persist.

segments.text: Big banks announced record-breaking earnings, fueled by cooling inflation and market activity, though analysts note looming "tectonic" risks.

sources.title: Cooling Inflation, Surging Bank Earnings Power Stocks
S&P 500 and Nasdaq end higher on cool inflation data, solid bank earnings
Big Banks Smash Earnings Records, but ‘Tectonic’ Risks Loom
Big banks poised to report booming revenue propelled by SpaceX IPO, Iran war volatility

bullets.lead: IBM's revenue miss signals caution for CIOs regarding AI spending.

segments.text: IBM's recent revenue miss suggests that CIOs may be re-evaluating their AI spending, indicating a potential shift in enterprise AI investment patterns.

sources.title: What IBM's Revenue Miss Tells CIOs About AI Spending

watchlist.name: SK Hynix
watchlist.note: Strong HBM outlook and ADR performance suggest continued investor interest.

watchlist.note: H200 shipments to China and Rubin V PCB bottlenecks will be key drivers.

watchlist.note: Market will continue to monitor inflation data and Fed commentary for rate policy signals.

watchlist.note: Geopolitical developments in the Middle East and US policy shifts continue to impact crude benchmarks.

watchlist.name: Apple

sources.title: Apple Suing OpenAI for Stealing Trade Secrets, Former Meta Director of AI on Muse Spark — TITV [Video]

x_themes.topic: Meta's 'Iris' AI chip production and compute expansion
x_themes.summary: Meta plans to produce its 'Iris' AI chip from September, aiming to double compute to 14GW next year, with Broadcom as chief design partner and TSMC manufacturing. Meta has also secured long-term supply agreements with Samsung (memory), SanDisk (flash storage), and Sumitomo (fiber optic).

x_themes.topic: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft
x_themes.summary: Apple has reportedly sued OpenAI for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to upcoming Apple products, accusing OpenAI of encouraging employees to share confidential information for its own device development.

x_themes.topic: Nvidia Rubin V PCB bottleneck and Victory Giant issues
x_themes.summary: Nvidia's Rubin V is facing a PCB bottleneck due to Victory Giant's HDI PCB issues, specifically an impedance mismatch degrading signal integrity, which could delay resolution until late July or later.

An analysis argues that AI is not a bubble, contrasting current tech investment and profitability trends with the dot-com era's diverging investment and eroding profit shares, while also addressing valuation, market concentration, and circular financing concerns.

x_themes.topic: Apple's accelerated M-series chip roadmap for AI
x_themes.summary: Apple is expected to launch a base M6 this fall, skip Pro/Max/Ultra, and move directly to M7 in H1 2027, accelerating due to significant Neural Engine AI upgrades, with M7 Ultra supporting up to 1.5TB memory and M8 chips planned for 2028 on 1.4nm.

x_themes.topic: TSMC vs. Samsung in CPO and advanced packaging
x_themes.summary: TSMC is noted as leading in switch CPO, while Samsung is advancing its 2.xD packaging, which integrates HBM, logic, and silicon photonics on an interposer, aiming for a design win binding these components within twelve months.
TSMC plans to hike prices on mature-process chip manufacturing by a single-digit percent in Q4, effective January, driven by AI boom demand for chips like PMICs.

x_themes.topic: OpenAI's planned consumer AI device launch
x_themes.summary: OpenAI's first consumer AI device, potentially a smart speaker, smart glasses, or wearable pin, could launch as early as February, though its timeline might be disrupted by the ongoing dispute with Apple.

x_themes.topic: Nanya Technology's 2027 capex for new DRAM fab
x_themes.summary: Nanya Technology plans to spend NT$200 billion (US$6.2 billion) on 2027 capex, aiming for 30,000 wpm capacity by 2028 at its new fab, part of a NT$480B project.

x_themes.topic: Samsung accelerates Yongin semiconductor cluster fab operations
x_themes.summary: Samsung has accelerated the start of operations for the first fab in its Yongin semiconductor cluster by two years, now targeting October 2029 for the first of six planned fabs.

x_themes.topic: IBK Research report on Boston Dynamics humanoid robotics value chain
x_themes.summary: An IBK Research report maps suppliers for Boston Dynamics' Atlas robot (Hwashin, LG Energy, Hyundai Autoever, Mobis) and models humanoid volume ramps, highlighting actuator capacity as a key indicator for 2028-2030 growth.

x_themes.topic: Low-DK Gen 2 PCB demand surge and resilient pricing
x_themes.summary: Demand for Low-DK Gen 2 PCBs is surging starting 2H26 due to new GPU and ASIC chips, leading to incredibly resilient pricing and potential further increases despite supply chain expansion challenges and tough yield issues.

x_themes.topic: CPO system constraints and timeline drivers
x_themes.summary: CPO's volume ramp is constrained by energy, heat, and reach, with its timeline dependent on the readiness of 800V power, liquid cooling, external-laser reliability, and optical-engine qualification, highlighting the interplay of light, electricity, and heat.

x_themes.topic: SK Hynix ADR performance and potential Samsung ADR listing
x_themes.summary: SK Hynix ADR ($SKYHV) is up 16% from listing, suggesting investors may have prepared funds for the US instrument, with expectations for the Korean market to rally and questions arising about a potential Samsung Electronics ADR listing.
